name: 01 — Deploy Watcher (Vercel → Auto-rollback)
description: Listens for Vercel deploy webhook. If failed deploy, triggers auto-rollback via Vercel API. Logs to Supabase. Emails PBS.

modules_overview.purpose: Receive Vercel webhook (deployment.error or deployment.canceled events)
modules_overview.config_notes: Create Custom Webhook in Make.com. Copy URL. Paste in Vercel → Project → Settings → Git → Deploy Hooks (Outbound).
Route 1: error. Route 2: canceled. Route 3: succeeded (used for logging only).

modules_overview.purpose: Vercel API: get current production deployment

modules_overview.config_notes: POST https://api.vercel.com/v13/deployments/{{previous_deployment_id}}/promote — only if step 1 was deployment.error AND production was affected

modules_overview.purpose: Log incident with auto_resolved=true if rollback succeeded
modules_overview.config_notes: Fields: severity=1, symptom='deploy failed', root_cause from Vercel error, fix='rolled back to {{previous_id}}', auto_resolved=true|false
Subject: 🚨 Deploy failed — auto-action: {{action}}. Body: deployment id, error, what was attempted, current status, link to Vercel.

secrets_needed: VERCEL_TOKEN (Vercel → Account Settings → Tokens, scoped to project)
manual_setup_steps: 4. Go to Vercel Project Settings → Git → Deploy Hooks
5. Add outbound webhook with Make.com URL
6. Test: trigger a failed deploy intentionally (push broken code to a branch, deploy)
9. Test rollback path (will need a real deploy to roll back)
guardrails: Only auto-rollback if previous deployment exists AND was successful
Max 3 auto-rollbacks per 24h — after that email PBS without rollback
Skip auto-rollback if deployment was on a branch, not production
Always log to Supabase even if no auto-action taken
